body:
- [ ] Create `LMUPI/lmupi/streaming_client.py` — QThread-based streaming client that:
  - Connects to driver server via TCP to receive channel map (handshake)
  - Opens UDP listener for telemetry frames
  - Buffers 100ms of data to smooth jitter
  - Handles packet loss gracefully (sequence-based gap skipping)
  - Auto-reconnects on disconnect with exponential backoff
  - Emits signals for connection status (connected/disconnected/error/status)
  - Feeds received data directly to LiveDashboard via `push()`
- [ ] Integrate streaming client into `dashboard.py` — Add "Connect Stream" button and connection dialog (IP:port input) alongside existing "Connect LMU" button
- [ ] Add unit tests for the streaming client protocol logic (serialization, buffering, packet loss handling)
- [ ] Run code review and security checks

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>Implement telemetry streaming client (engineer side)</issue_title>
> <issue_description>## Description
> Create a client that receives streamed telemetry and feeds it to the dashboard.
> 
> ## Requirements
> - Connect to driver server by IP:port
> - TCP handshake to receive channel map
> - UDP listener for telemetry frames
> - Feed received data directly to LiveDashboard
> - Handle packet loss gracefully (skip gaps, don't stall)
> - Connection status display
> - Auto-reconnect on disconnect with backoff
> - Buffer 100ms of data to smooth jitter
> 
> ## Depends On
> - Streaming protocol spec (#16)
> - Streaming server (#17)</issue_description>
> 
> ## Comments on the Issue (you are @copilot in this section)
> 
> <comments>
> </comments>
> 


</details>

- Fixes SeamusMullan/TeleMU#18
